title: smoky algerian flour brik
description: a sweet and light algerian pastry, perfect for dessert or snack time, made with strong wholemeal flour and a smoky hot filling.
instructions: in a large bowl, combine the wholemeal flour and warm water to make the dough.
knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
flatten each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
in a small bowl, mix together the sugar, smoked paprika, almond milk, apple cider vinegar, coconut oil, vanilla extract, and lemon zest to make the filling.
place a tablespoon of the filling in the center of each dough circle.
fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal the brik.
heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat and cook the briks for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
serve the briks warm, dust with powdered sugar if desired.
